Tier 3 — Local real-time delivery. Gas peakers are marginal-cost units dispatched when baseload (nuclear/hydro) is insufficient. Output >3,000 MW = demand stress; >5,000 MW = grid under significant pressure. Elevated gas dispatch correlates with higher wholesale electricity prices and increased exposure to Tier 1/2 commodity risk. Hour ending 24:00 UTC.

Futures price (~15 min delay). Tier 1 - Global commodity. ICE Brent crude: global benchmark for ~2/3 of world oil trade. Ontario supply chain: Brent (Tier 1) -> TCPL pipeline utilization (Tier 2) -> Toronto pump price / IESO gas dispatch (Tier 3). CAD/USD amplifies or dampens pass-through to Ontario consumers. Warn >$80/bbl (refinery margin pressure), Alert >$100/bbl (Hormuz disruption threshold). Source: Yahoo Finance ICE Brent front-month futures (BZ=F), near-real-time.

Enbridge Dawn: No capacity constraints on all monitored paths. Distribution areas: 76G/0Y/0R across 76 path-days (4-day outlook). Last updated: May 28, 2026. Key S&T paths for GTA supply: Dawn→Parkway (primary corridor), Dawn→Kirkwall, Kirkwall→Parkway. States: Green=No constraints, Yellow=Interruptible impacted, Red=Firm services impacted. Signals: Traffic signal TDs: 76 all-green.

Dawn + Tecumseh working storage: 120 PJ (113.7 Bcf, 38.1% of 315 PJ working capacity). Previous period: 153 PJ (145.0 Bcf). Change: -33 PJ (▼ withdrawing). Season: injection season (Apr–Sep) — post-winter refill underway. Thresholds — Warn: <63 PJ, Alert: <32 PJ. Ontario gas supply chain: Brent crude (Tier 1) → TCPL pipelines (Tier 2) → Dawn storage + Enbridge distribution (Tier 2) → GTA consumers (Tier 3). Dawn is Canada's largest integrated underground storage facility (~33 pools). Published twice monthly. Source: https://www.enbridgegas.com/storage-transportation/operational-information/storage-reporting
results.threshold_note: Storage below 142 PJ (45% capacity) — reduced winter buffer

results.notes: Tier 3 — Local real-time delivery. Regular unleaded, includes all taxes. Daily forecast from commodity markets. Supply chain: Brent crude (Tier 1) → TCPL pipeline utilization (Tier 2) → Toronto pump price (Tier 3). CAD/USD rate amplifies or dampens pass-through. Source: stockr.net (Canadian, daily at 11am). Warn >150¢/L, Alert >185¢/L.

NOTAM APIs for Canadian airports require browser sessions — no free automated source. Manual check: plan.navcanada.ca → Weather & NOTAM tab → enter CYYZ → NOTAMs. Severity scale: 0=Normal, 1=Reduced capacity (approach U/S), 2=Runway closure, 3=Flow control or security restriction. Also check: flightaware.com/live/airport/CYYZ for live delay status.

No live open data feed available. Current ridership in monthly KPI PDFs: ttc.ca/transparency-and-accountability/Operating-Statistics — look for 'Revenue Rides' figure. Toronto Open Data only has 1985-2019 historical XLSX. Will auto-fetch if a live resource appears on the package.

TTC subway: Normal service on all subway lines. 8 subway alert(s) (0 unplanned, 8 planned). API updated: 2026-06-02T23:41 (4.1h ago). Lines 1–4 (Yonge-University, Bloor-Danforth, Scarborough, Sheppard). ~1.7M weekly riders. Unplanned Line 1/2 suspension = Tier-1 mobility event.

Service alerts active (3 found). GO Transit: 7 rail lines + UP Express, ~70,000 weekday GTHA riders. Severity: 0=Normal, 1=Minor alerts, 2=Major disruption. Source: gotransit.com/en/service-updates (scraped).
Active service alerts detected. Check gotransit.com for line-specific details.

CN: Baseline — dedicated bargaining page exists but no active dispute content. Current collective agreement expires 2026-12-31. Next negotiation window opens ~6 months prior. Risk scale: 0=No dispute, 1=Active negotiations, 2=Imminent/active stoppage. A CN/CP simultaneous stoppage halts ~$1B/day in Canadian trade and affects GO Transit commuter lines sharing CN track. Federal government has historically intervened within hours under Canada Labour Code s.107. Source: https://www.cn.ca/en/media/bargaining-updates/

CPKC: Baseline — page content minimal (JS-rendered or no active updates). Current collective agreement expires 2026-12-31. Next negotiation window opens ~6 months prior. Risk scale: 0=No dispute, 1=Active negotiations, 2=Imminent/active stoppage. A CN/CP simultaneous stoppage halts ~$1B/day in Canadian trade and affects GO Transit commuter lines sharing CN track. Federal government has historically intervened within hours under Canada Labour Code s.107. Source: https://www.cpkcr.com/en/media/news-releases

results.source: Toronto Police Service — Task Force Guardian
